channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: NASA astronaut reveals they nearly failed to dock Boeing Starliner to International Space Station
item.description: NASA astronaut Butch Wilmore revealed that the Boeing Starliner almost had to abort its attempt to dock to the International Space Station on June 5, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: If passed, the Trade Review Act of 2025 would require the president to notify lawmakers of an imposition or increase in tariffs within 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: US electric vehicle industry is collateral damage in Trump's escalating trade war
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — President Donald Trump's tariff blitz has sent shock waves throughout every aspect of the global economy, including the auto sector, where multi-billion-dollar plans to electrify in the United States are especially at risk.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Iowa student’s passport seizure in Dominican Republic raises red flags for Americans traveling: What to know
item.description: Joshua Riibe was forced to turn over his passport to Dominican authorities in the search for Sudiksha Konanki, a move that travel safety experts warn could be problematic for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires NSC officials a day after far-right activist raises concerns to him about staff loyalty
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he's fired "some" White House National Security Council officials, a move that comes a day after far-right activist Laura Loomer raised concerns directly to him about staff loyalty. Trump downplayed Loomer's influence on the firings.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South and Midwest face potentially catastrophic rains and floods while reeling from tornadoes
item.description: Parts of the Midwest and South are facing the possibility torrential rains and life-threatening flash floods on Friday The fresh storms come as many communities are still reeling from severe tornadoes that destroyed whole neighborhoods and killed at least seven people.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz, George Walker Iv And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: SENECA, Kansas (AP) — A Catholic priest was shot and killed Thursday by a man who approached him at his parish rectory in the town of Seneca, Kansas, church officials said. “I am heartbroken to share the tragic news of the death of Fr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to find out if your car is foreign-made
item.description: President Donald Trump recently announced a 25% tariff on all foreign-made vehicles and their parts—including those used in U.S. assembly plants.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains of Illinois woman who disappeared in 1983 found after cold case investigators start podcast
item.description: Karen Schepers' remains were finally identified after being found in a car submerged in the Fox River, nearly 43 years after she went missing, thanks to the efforts of the Elgin Police Department and the non-profit organization Chaos Divers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC staff working on childhood lead exposure, cancer clusters fired
item.description: The permanent staff of the Division of Environmental Health Science and Practice was cut, according to one source.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves prompt emergency in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to declare a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Security Council staffers fired in wake of Trump meeting with Laura Loomer
item.description: Multiple staff members on the White House National Security Council were fired Thursday following a meeting President Trump had with far-right activist Laura Loomer where she raised questions about their loyalty.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Tony's Chocolonely issues recall after 'small stones' found in chocolate bars
item.description: Tony's Chocolonely Inc. issued a voluntary recall for its Tony's brand Dark Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar because the products may contain small stones. The two bars were sold nationwide via retail stores and www.tonyschocolonely.com. The product was distributed from Feb. 7 to March 24.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: National Burrito Day deals to look for Thursday
item.description: (KRON) -- Thursday is National Burrito Day. The day is typically celebrated on the first Thursday in April, April 3 this year, and celebrates everyone's appreciation for the popular dish, which has been offered in American restaurants since the 1930s.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Missing Wisconsin teen found more than two months after disappearance
item.description: Sophia Franklin, 16, of Wisconsin was found safe in Nebraska after two months on the run with Gary Day, who is also suspected of being the father of Franklin's child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Girl, 7, struck, killed by falling boulder at Lake Tahoe ski resort
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was killed by a falling boulder at a popular ski resort in Lake Tahoe over the weekend, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Bodega cats make New Yorkers' hearts purr, even if they violate state regulations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City’s “bodega cats” are beloved fixtures in the Big Apple — but they’re on the wrong side of the law.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams will skip the Democratic primary and run for reelection as an independent
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he will pull out of the city’s Democratic primary and will instead run for reelection as an independent candidate.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms struck parts of the South and Midwest on Wednesday, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, KRON4

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his physical condition resembled someone "at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student detained by ICE moved to Vermont before judge's order, government says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities had been moved to Vermont by the time a federal judge ordered authorities to keep her in Massachusetts, lawyers for the U.S. government said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Mass layoffs rattle Head Start leaders already on edge over funding problems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The problems for Head Start began days after President Donald Trump took office. Trump's administration announced it would freeze federal grants — the primary funding for the early education program that serves more than half a million low-income children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs plan: What we know - and don't
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday is set to unveil reciprocal tariffs on several nations during a White House event. He has dubbed April 2 as "Liberation Day" in honor of these tariffs, with the ceremony being promoted as an effort to "make America wealthy again.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court. But a Boston Municipal Court judge issued a ruling Monday against Sullivan, arguing that he had deprived Martell-Lebron of his rights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5
